texto: Wouldn’t this old world be better If the folks we meet would say: ‘I know something good about you,’ And then treat us that way?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de N. Eldon Tanner que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “Wouldn’t this old world be better If the folks we meet would say: ‘I know something good about yo…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, kindness, wouldn, com foco em reflexao.

texto: A mother has far greater influence on her children than anyone else, and she must realize that every word she speaks, every act, every response, her attitude, even her appearance and manner of dress affect the lives of her children and the whole family. It is while the child is in the home that he gains from his mother the attitudes, hopes, and beliefs that will determine the kind of life he will live and the contribution he will make to society.
explicacao: Orientação direta de N. Eldon Tanner sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “A mother has far greater influence on her children than anyone else, and she must realize that ev…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, motherhood, reflexao, útil em momentos de reflexao.
